---
title: "Project 1 Draft"
---

| | |
|---|---|
| **Project** | Project 1: Linear Regression |
| **Due** | Week 9 |
| **Weight** | 30% of Project 1 (which is 20% of final grade) |
| **Related Guide** | [Project Guide]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} |

::: {.callout-tip}
## {{< fa book >}} Project Guide
Use the [Project 1 guidelines]({{< var books.project_guide >}}/chapters/ch02-guidelines-linear.html){target="_blank"}, [worked Project 1 proposal]({{< var books.project_guide >}}/chapters/ch04-linear-proposal.html){target="_blank"}, and [worked Project 1 results chapter]({{< var books.project_guide >}}/chapters/ch05-linear-results.html){target="_blank"} while drafting. Before submitting, compare your draft with the [Project 1 checklist]({{< var books.project_guide >}}/chapters/ch02-guidelines-linear.html#sec-linear-checklist){target="_blank"}.
:::

## Purpose

The draft is a complete working version of your linear regression analysis. It should include a working version of Parts 1--6 of the 7-part final report, plus any preliminary appendix or reproducibility materials needed to support your diagnostics. The writing may still be rough. The instructor will provide feedback to help you strengthen the analysis and interpretation before the final submission.

## Requirements

Your draft should include all of the following sections. These are a milestone checklist; the Project Guide remains the detailed source for the final 7-part report structure.

### 1. Introduction

- Research question (refined from your proposal, incorporating any instructor feedback)
- Brief motivation for why this question matters
- Overview of your analytical approach

### 2. Data and Variables

- Dataset description (updated from proposal)
- Variable definitions and coding
- Descriptive statistics and exploratory visualizations

### 3. Analysis and Results

- **Model specification**: State the regression model(s) you are fitting
- **Regression output**: Report coefficient estimates, standard errors, and significance tests
- **Model fit**: Report $R^2$ and any other relevant fit statistics
- **Interpretation**: Interpret key coefficients in context
- **Additional analyses** (if applicable): Interactions, multiple comparisons, etc.

### 4. Model Diagnostics

- Residual plots (residuals vs. fitted, Q-Q plot)
- Assessment of key assumptions (linearity, normality, homoscedasticity)
- Identification of any influential observations

### 5. Preliminary Conclusions

- Summary of main findings
- Limitations you have identified so far

## Submission

Submit the following to Blackboard:

- A rendered PDF or HTML document
- Your `.qmd` source file
- The dataset (or a link to it)
